title: Editas Medicine Reports Q1 2024 Results and Updates
text: Editas Medicine reported Q1 2024 financial results with net loss of $62.0 million compared to $49.0 million in Q1 2023. Reported EPS of -$0.76, missing expectations of -$0.63. Research and development expenses increased by $11.0 million to $48.8 million, primarily due to higher sublicense and license payments and clinical and manufacturing costs for reni-cel program. General and administrative expenses decreased by $3.7 million to $19.3 million due to lower professional service expenses and reduced legal and patent costs. As of March 31, 2024, company held $376.8 million in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities, expected to fund operations into 2026. RUBY trial for sickle cell disease completed adult cohort enrollment and began adolescent cohort enrollment with comprehensive clinical data set expected mid-2024 and further updates by year-end. EdiTHAL trial for beta thalassemia continued enrollment with additional data updates planned for mid-2024.
